claimText: The Central Asian Mammals Initiative (CAMI) aims to coordinate conservation efforts for 17 key migratory mammal species, including the Saiga and Bukhara Deer.
evidenceExcerpt: CAMI aims to coordinate conservation efforts for 17 key migratory mammal species, including the Saiga and Bukhara Deer.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the target species and scope of CAMI.
distractorHooks: Targeting birds or marine species instead of mammals
Different flagship species like Snow Leopard or Tiger
Initiative of South Asian or Southeast Asian countries

claimText: CAMI was launched in 2014 at the 11th Meeting of the Conference of the Parties (COP11) to the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals (CMS).
evidenceExcerpt: It was launched in 2014 under the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals (CMS) at COP11.
examUse: Statement-based question on the parent convention and origin of CAMI.
distractorHooks: Launched under CITES or CBD instead of CMS
